title: Ageratina glauca
taxonomy: Ageratina glauca is classified in the family Asteraceae, also known as the sunflower or daisy family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many common species. Ageratina glauca is further classified in the genus Ageratina, which includes various species of shrubs and small trees native to the Americas. The genus Ageratina is part of the tribe Eupatorieae, a group of plants known for their clusters of small flowers. Ageratina glauca's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Ageratina glauca, commonly known as the tree ageratina or tree shrub, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. intro: Ageratina glauca, also known as Blue Mist Flower, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina glauca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ageratina glauca is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in herbal supplements and topical creams.
